description.content: Operations for creating and managing Aurora database clusters

item.name: Amazon RDS Create a new DB cluster

request.name: Amazon RDS Create a new DB cluster

description.content: Creates a new Amazon Aurora DB cluster or Multi-AZ DB cluster. You can use the ReplicationSourceIdentifier parameter to create an Amazon Aurora DB cluster as a read replica of another DB cluster or Amazon RDS MySQL or PostgreSQL DB instance.

query.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier, unique for each DB cluster
(Required) The database engine to use (aurora-mysql, aurora-postgresql)
The version number of the database engine to use
The name of the master user for the DB cluster
The password for the master database user
The name for a database to create in the DB cluster
The port number on which the instances in the DB cluster accept connections
A DB subnet group to associate with this DB cluster
A VPC security group to associate with the DB cluster
Whether the DB cluster is encrypted
The number of days for which automated backups are retained (1-35)

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Returns information about Amazon Aurora DB clusters and Multi-AZ DB clusters. This API supports pagination. For more information on Amazon Aurora DB clusters, see the Amazon Aurora User Guide.

query.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to describe
The name of a filter
A value for the filter
Maximum number of records to include in the response
An optional pagination token provided by a previous request

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB cluster

request.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB cluster

description.content: Deletes a previously provisioned DB cluster. When you delete a DB cluster, all automated backups are deleted and cannot be recovered. Manual DB cluster snapshots are not deleted.

query.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier for the DB cluster to be deleted
Whether to skip the creation of a final DB cluster snapshot before deleting the cluster
The identifier of the final DB cluster snapshot to create before deleting the cluster

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Operations for describing available database engine versions

item.name: Amazon RDS Describe DB engine versions

request.name: Amazon RDS Describe DB engine versions

description.content: Returns a list of the available DB engines and their versions. You can filter by engine, engine version, DB parameter group family, and other criteria.

query.description: The database engine to return (mysql, postgres, mariadb, oracle-ee, sqlserver-ee, aurora-mysql, aurora-postgresql)
The database engine version to return
The name of a specific DB parameter group family to return details for
Whether to return only the default version of the engine
Maximum number of records to include in the response
An optional pagination token provided by a previous request

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Operations for creating, managing, and deleting RDS database instances

item.name: Amazon RDS Create a new DB instance

request.name: Amazon RDS Create a new DB instance

description.content: Creates a new DB instance. The new DB instance can be an RDS DB instance, or it can be a DB instance in an Aurora DB cluster. You can specify the DB engine, instance class, allocated storage, master username and password, and other configuration settings.

query.description: (Required) The DB instance identifier, unique for each DB instance
(Required) The compute and memory capacity of the DB instance (e.g., db.m5.large)
(Required) The database engine to use (mysql, postgres, mariadb, oracle-ee, sqlserver-ee, aurora-mysql, aurora-postgresql)
The name for the master user
The password for the master user
The amount of storage in gibibytes (GiB) to allocate for the DB instance
The version number of the database engine to use
The name of the database to create when the DB instance is created
The port number on which the database accepts connections
Whether the DB instance is a Multi-AZ deployment
The storage type to associate with the DB instance (gp2, gp3, io1, standard)
Whether the DB instance is publicly accessible
A VPC security group to associate with the DB instance
A DB subnet group to associate with this DB instance

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Describes provisioned RDS instances. This API supports pagination. Returns information about the DB instances including engine, instance class, storage, endpoint, status, and configuration details.

query.description: The identifier of the DB instance to describe
The name of a filter
A value for the filter
Maximum number of records to include in the response
An optional pagination token provided by a previous request

item.name: Amazon RDS Modify a DB instance

request.name: Amazon RDS Modify a DB instance

description.content: Modifies settings for a DB instance. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying these parameters and the new values in the request. Some modifications are applied immediately, while others are applied during the next maintenance window.

query.description: (Required) The DB instance identifier for the instance to be modified
The new compute and memory capacity of the DB instance
The new amount of storage in GiB to allocate for the DB instance
The new password for the master user
Whether the DB instance is a Multi-AZ deployment
The version number of the database engine to upgrade to
Whether to apply the modification immediately or during the next maintenance window
The number of days to retain automated backups (0-35)
The storage type to associate with the DB instance

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

item.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB instance

request.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB instance

description.content: Deletes a previously provisioned DB instance. When you delete a DB instance, all automated backups are deleted and cannot be recovered. Manual DB snapshots are not deleted.

query.description: (Required) The DB instance identifier for the DB instance to be deleted
Whether to skip the creation of a final DB snapshot before deleting the instance
The identifier of the final DB snapshot to create before deleting the instance
Whether to remove automated backups immediately after deletion

item.name: Amazon RDS Reboot a DB instance

request.name: Amazon RDS Reboot a DB instance

description.content: Reboots a DB instance. The reboot causes a momentary outage and the database instance status is set to rebooting. A DB instance can be rebooted when it is in the available state. If the DB instance is in a Multi-AZ deployment, you can force a failover from one AZ to another.

query.description: (Required) The DB instance identifier
Whether to force a failover from one AZ to another in a Multi-AZ deployment

Operations for creating and managing database snapshots

item.name: Amazon RDS Create a DB snapshot

request.name: Amazon RDS Create a DB snapshot

description.content: Creates a snapshot of a DB instance. The source DB instance must be in the available or storage-optimization state. The DB snapshot is created with a snapshot type of manual.

query.description: (Required) The identifier for the DB snapshot
(Required) The identifier of the DB instance to create the snapshot from

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The ID of the DB instance to retrieve snapshots for
A specific DB snapshot identifier to describe
The type of snapshots to return (automated, manual, shared, public, awsbackup)
Maximum number of records to include in the response
An optional pagination token provided by a previous request

item.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB snapshot

request.name: Amazon RDS Delete a DB snapshot

description.content: Deletes a DB snapshot. If the snapshot is being copied, the copy operation is terminated. The DB snapshot must be in the available state to be deleted.

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB snapshot to delete

AWS Region

Amazon Relational Database Service (RDS) makes it easy to set up, operate, and scale a relational database in the cloud. This API enables you to create and manage database instances, clusters, snapshots, and other database resources with support for multiple database engines including MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, and Amazon Aurora.
